scenarios.scenario-1: Apex and wildcard certificates exist at stable mount paths and are readable by the app container.
scenarios.scenario-1b: When REG.RU credentials are present, DNS-01 is completed automatically without interactive TXT entry.
scenarios.scenario-1c: An imported certificate pair is staged into the same stable mount paths and remains readable by the app container.
scenarios.scenario-1d: An already issued certificate pair can be exported from one VPS and imported on another without invoking a new Let’s Encrypt issuance flow.
scenarios.scenario-2: DNS challenge prerequisites are missing or TXT propagation is not yet visible, and the installer keeps the operator in the guided wait loop instead of continuing blindly.
scenarios.scenario-3: Renewal failure is visible before live traffic breaks.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: Wildcard certificate flow is explicit and not silently downgraded.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Certificate material persists across container restarts.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Guided manual DNS-01 does not continue until the requested TXT value is publicly visible.

side-effects.effect-1: Writes certificate and renewal files to host storage.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Do not log provider credentials, tokens, or private key material.

verification-levels.module-local: Challenge selection and path rendering checks.
verification-levels.wave-level: Real certificate issuance on a disposable domain or staging endpoint.
verification-levels.phase-level: 443 edge serves valid TLS for portal and proxy routing.
